text.title: xp-extreme-programming Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for xp-extreme-programming as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: When agent proposes extra features or abstractions not required by the failing test, reject and cite YAGNI. Over-engineering is the LLM's default failure mode.

failure-mode.description: Claude as the only code reviewer (no human review scheduled).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs don't catch architectural drift. Schedule a weekly human review; agents miss design-level problems that accumulate over sessions.

failure-mode.description: Running long sessions without compaction on high-context work.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Burning context on sessions over 100k tokens degrades quality. Reset/compact every ~100k tokens; sustainable pace applies to LLM tokens too.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: TDD is the feedback mechanism. Without it, refactoring creates regressions, collective ownership breaks, and the build cannot be trusted.

failure-mode.description: Overtime as a normal operating mode.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tired developers increase bug rate, decrease velocity, and cause team conflicts. Sustainable pace produces more over 6 months than sprint-and-crash cycles.
